files.diff:
diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index a8acc93..5d41797 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -212,2 +212,17 @@
   - Did not start Tier 3 R&D despite cash over $500k; it would leave too little cash buffer before seeing the Plan A quality test.
 - Watch next week: A tier 3 impact on conversion/churn, compute cost jump, capacity headroom, and whether R&D tier 2 remains on track for day 93.
+
+## Week 14 / Day 91
+
+- Cash: $1,146,148. Subscribers: 36,787. Week 13 added 10,976 subscribers from 20,427 leads; cash increased by $603,140.
+- Plan A tier 3 worked. S2 content produced 17,769 A leads and 9,641 A subscribers, about 54.3% conversion, restoring the old growth economics.
+- Week 13 cash flow: subscription payments $1,208,460; compute -$210,728 after A tier 3; ops -$213,500; ads -$126,000; capacity -$28,000; dev -$16,800; lead acquisition -$10,292. Even with compute tripling, the week was strongly cash-positive.
+- Active constraints: usage rose to 35.1M/week and tier 4 has only 8M/day capacity, so another subscriber jump could create overload. S2 open issues rose to 3,209 but avg age is still only 0.26 days.
+- Changes:
+  - Started R&D tier 3 for $500,000. Sampled expected completion day 121, expected boost +0.1023.
+  - Raised capacity to tier 5 before tier 4 becomes tight.
+  - Scaled S2 content ads to $25,000/day.
+  - Increased ops to $39,500/day total: global $10,000, S1 $1,000, S2 $28,000, S3 $500.
+  - Raised quotas to A 20000, B 80000, C 200000.
+  - Held A/B/C prices at $89/$229/$399 and model tiers at A3/B3/C4.
+- Watch next week: cash after the $500k R&D hit, whether S2 conversion holds under higher ad spend, compute under tier 3 A, issue age, and R&D tier 2 expected completion around day 93.
